seoTitle: How to Add a Trend Line Using Power BI's Analytics Pane
metaDescription: Add a trend line to a Power BI chart using the Analytics pane to spot patterns and support forecasting in your reports.
intro: The Analytics pane in Power BI makes it simple to add a trend line to a chart without any manual calculation, helping you spot patterns and support forecasting. This guide walks through adding and customizing a trend line using that built-in tool. It's a quick technique that adds real analytical value to sales, marketing, or performance charts.

steps.heading: Open the Analytics pane
steps.body: Select your chart and navigate to the Analytics pane, which is where trend line options live in Power BI.

steps.heading: Add a trend line
steps.body: Turn on the trend line option within the Analytics pane so Power BI calculates and displays it directly on your chart.

steps.heading: Customize the trend line
steps.body: Adjust the trend line's color, style, or transparency so it's clearly distinguishable from your actual data points.

steps.heading: Use trend lines for business analysis
steps.body: Apply the trend line to scenarios like forecasting revenue or analyzing campaign performance over time.

steps.heading: Combine with automation and AI integration
steps.body: Pair trend line analysis with automated data refreshes so your forecasts stay current as new data comes in.

faq.question: Does Power BI automatically calculate the trend line for me?
faq.answer: Yes, once enabled through the Analytics pane, Power BI calculates the trend line based on your chart's existing data.

faq.question: Can I add a trend line to any chart type?
faq.answer: Trend lines are commonly available on line and scatter charts, though availability can vary depending on the visual type.

faq.question: Is the trend line the same as a forecast?
faq.answer: A trend line shows the general direction of existing data, while a full forecast projects future values, which is a related but separate feature.

faq.question: Can sales teams use trend lines to predict revenue?
faq.answer: Yes, trend lines are commonly used by sales teams to visualize direction and support revenue forecasting discussions.

faq.question: Will adding a trend line change my underlying data?
faq.answer: No, a trend line is a visual overlay and doesn't modify the actual data points in your report.
